title: kohautia cuspidata
intro: Kohautia cuspidata, also known as African boxwood, is a shrub-like plant native to tropical Africa, characterized by its small, green leaves and woody stems. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, making it effective in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Kohautia cuspidata include relief from fever, headache, and skin infections, as well as a reduction in inflammation and pain. Kohautia cuspidata is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, where the leaves and stems are steeped in hot water to release its medicinal compounds, and can also be used in traditional herbal preparations.
